title: Political, Religious, and Love Poems (U-M encoded selection, pp. xlv-292)
summary: The repository artifact is a bounded structurally enriched copy of the raw U-M encoding delivered in an Internet Archive Raw Michigan Book carrier. That carrier separately contains the encoding and a complete 408-TIFF facsimile of the 1903 Furnivall volume. The edition transcribes, collates, cites, or consults twenty-seven manuscript witnesses plus earlier printed/scholarly comparators; all facsimile, carrier, access, and rights layers remain distinct.

subject.description: Structurally enriched repository XML for the encoded selection

scope.notes: A bounded normalized comparison confirms identical 14,055-element structures, identical 358 PB mappings, 118 direct DIV2 containers, and 16 DIV3 containers. The repository copy adds 140 DIV1/DIV2/DIV3 NODE attributes plus IDs on 1,954 NOTE1 and 4 NOTE2 elements (1,958 total) and changes EDITORIALDECL N from 1 to 4. Reversing exactly those generated changes and ignoring formatting-only whitespace yields zero structural/serialized differences.

editorial_practices.description: The electronic text retains the 1903 title page, a correction note, and pp. xlv-292, while omitting other front matter, pp. 293-342, and back matter.
Furnivall identifies base witnesses and collates or consults alternate witnesses and earlier printed comparators for selected items.
editorial_practices.scope: Items whose edition headings or notes name collation/consultation

editorial_practices.representation: Heavy/thick type in the printed edition
editorial_practices.description: The notes to items 4.4 and 4.34 say heavy or thick letters mark red manuscript lettering.

evidence.summary: The exact repository source object.

evidence.citation: U-M CME TEI header embedded in ant9912.xml
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1903 source edition, U-M identifier, encoding date, Level 1 OCR status, language, and rights metadata.

evidence.citation: Computational and manual inspection of ant9912.xml
evidence.summary: Establishes the selected-page boundary, 118 DIV2 count axis, 16 DIV3 analytical children, forms, witnesses, and edition notes.
Identifies the exact 1903 EETS Original Series 15 volume and public-domain-US full-view copies.
Separates the Rossetti notes and the later romance material from the pp. xlv-292 U-M selection.
Complete 1866 first-edition scan.
Identifies the 1822 black-letter reprint used for item 4.7.
Identifies public full-view page images of the complete 1822 carrier; MARC 300 explicitly describes its core as a reprint.
Identifies the c. 1476 Caxton print issue represented by PML 27473 and traces this exact copy from York Minster to the Morgan.

evidence.citation: William Blades, The Biography and Typography of William Caxton, England's First Printer, 2nd ed. (1882)
evidence.summary: Identifies the perfect York Cathedral copy of Caxton's second printing as the copy from which Sir Mark Masterman Sykes presented a reprint to the Roxburghe Club; combined with Morgan provenance, this identifies the historical copy now cataloged as PML 27473.
Complete Henslow 1899 volume; Furnivall compares item 5.74 against p. 125.

evidence.citation: Denise White, diplomatic transcription of Cotton Caligula A II (2012)
evidence.summary: Modern complete diplomatic transcription supporting inspection of the Cotton witness; it was not a source available to Furnivall.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item metadata for ant9912.0001.001.umich.edu
evidence.summary: Internet Archive identifies the original 26,095,303-byte Raw Michigan Book ZIP, its 413-member count, and its MD5/SHA-1/CRC32 fixity.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive Raw Michigan Book ZIP for U-M ANT9912.0001.001
evidence.locator: 413 archive members: 408 contiguous TIFFs plus XML, TXT, HDR, pageview.dat, and loadcd.log
evidence.summary: Complete carrier passes ZIP CRC and upstream MD5/SHA-1 checks. It contains all 408 TIFFs, including unencoded members 00000343-00000392 (printed pp. 293-342); target body refs are 00000047-00000342, with title/correction on 00000004-00000006.

evidence.citation: Bounded structural comparison of raw U-M XML with pinned General Corpus ant9912.xml
evidence.summary: Both files have 14,055 elements. After removing 140 generated DIV1/DIV2/DIV3 NODE attributes and IDs on 1,954 NOTE1 plus 4 NOTE2 elements, restoring EDITORIALDECL N from 4 to 1, dropping formatting-only whitespace nodes, and canonicalizing, the structures have zero differences (normalized SHA-256 796da310de69c63ed12c10028bf3c57243ae3d2c97ca33f5b5881cdcb7d52ba2).

open_questions.question: How many normalized abstract medieval works lie behind the 118 edition representation containers?
next_steps: Perform item-by-item work-identity normalization only in a later analytical project; retain 118 strictly as the edition-container count.

open_questions.question: Can anonymous component texts receive more granular authorship, composition dates, or places from later scholarship?
next_steps: Research individual containers against current scholarly catalogs and indexes without altering the frozen edition-container boundary.

open_questions.question: Is an anonymously downloadable complete PDF of the exact 1903 re-edition available?
next_steps: The complete raw U-M 408-TIFF package now satisfies whole-volume facsimile access; recheck HathiTrust and institutional repositories only for a convenient anonymous whole-volume PDF.

open_questions.question: Can full-color reproductions be obtained for the thirteen British Library witnesses without current public complete facsimiles?
next_steps: Submit shelfmark-specific requests through the current British Library reproduction form and obtain quotations/rights terms.

open_questions.question: Can a complete reproduction of Cambridge Hh.4.12 be obtained?
next_steps: Ask Cambridge Manuscripts staff for availability and a quote under current image-supply terms.

open_questions.question: Can complete reproductions of Rawlinson C. 228 and Bodley 42 be obtained?
next_steps: Use Bodleian mediated-copying guidance to request the target leaves or whole codices where feasible.

open_questions.question: Can Lambeth MS 853 pages 4-14 and 74-88, absent from the public image set, be obtained?
next_steps: Request the target page ranges or full codex from Lambeth Imaging Services and confirm publication terms.

open_questions.question: Can the last manuscript page of New College MS 320 containing the William Huchen hymn be obtained?
next_steps: Contact the New College Librarian and request that target page or a complete manuscript-section reproduction.

review_notes: Lambeth MS 853 public images are partial and target absent at entity, relation, access/local-copy, rights, and evidence scope.
New College MS 320 public selected images are partial and target absent; the last manuscript page must be requested.
Catalog entities describe witnesses but do not substitute for physical witnesses or facsimiles.
Harley 3810 is contextual/cited as another copy, not an immediate transcript source.
White 2012 is a later supporting transcription, not a source available to Furnivall.

The Internet Archive raw-package carrier, internal U-M encoding, complete 408-TIFF facsimile, and General Corpus repository XML are distinct layers; the raw-to-pinned copied_from claim is limited by the exact normalized structural audit.
